query: I keep repeating the same incident-response workflow in Codex. Turn it into a reusable skill with a SKILL.md, scripts folder if useful, and clear trigger wording.
Can you update my existing skill at /tmp/skills/pr-reviewer? It under-triggers when I ask for security-oriented PR reviews, and I want a better description plus a few eval prompts.
I have notes from a manual spreadsheet cleanup process. Build a Codex skill from them, include helper scripts if repeated parsing is needed, and validate the final skill folder.
Please compare two versions of this skill and tell me if the new one is actually better. I want test prompts, benchmark results, and a human review page.
The skill I wrote works only for my sample prompt. Help me generalize it, create proper evals, run with-skill and baseline passes, then iterate on the failures.
Package the local Codex skill in ./skills/docx-helper as a .skill file and make sure its frontmatter and metadata are valid first.
Add agents/openai.yaml to this skill. I need a display name, short description, icon paths, and a default prompt that matches the SKILL.md.
Create an eval set to test whether my skill description triggers for the right user requests but avoids near-miss tasks. Then run the optimizer for a few iterations.
Convert this internal runbook into a Codex skill, but keep the SKILL.md lean and move the long policy details into references files.
Forward-test this skill with realistic user requests. I want outputs saved per eval case and a viewer I can use to leave feedback.
Package my Python project as a wheel, update pyproject.toml metadata, and publish the build artifact to our private package index.
Write a stronger system prompt for a customer support bot. I do not need a Codex skill or any files, just the prompt text.
Run our model eval harness on the latest summarization outputs and plot accuracy over time. The evals are in tests/eval_summaries.jsonl.
Create a GitHub Actions workflow that validates YAML frontmatter in markdown docs across this repository.
Design a Codex plugin marketplace entry with plugin.json and README text for a local extension I am building.
I need a README for a folder named skills that explains our team's onboarding workshops, not Codex skills.
Optimize the description for this npm package so it ranks better in search results. It is not an AI skill.
Make a simple browser UI for reviewing CSV cleanup outputs. Use static HTML, but there is no skill evaluation workflow involved.
Audit the security of a helper script that zips folders. I am worried about path traversal in archive extraction.
Create a reusable shell script template for new repositories with lint, test, and format commands.
